commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=57cae484020c0a2c9432e38131bd4bd26388e50f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR strips the OpenClaw `## Skills` system-prompt section when a Claude CLI skills plugin is materialized, and adds helper plus spawn-path coverage.

PR surface: Source +28, Tests +56. Total +84 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main builds a `## Skills` prompt section from eligible skills and also materializes the same snapshot as a Claude CLI plugin. I did not run a live Claude CLI token-count smoke in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blockers are maintainer compatibility judgment, docs alignment, and contributor-supplied real behavior proof, not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(cli): avoid duplicate Claude skills prompt
target.body:
## Behavior or issue addressed

Fixes #87063.

Claude CLI already receives OpenClaw skills through the native Claude plugin path (`--plugin-dir`). The same run could also include the OpenClaw `<available_skills>` catalog in the system prompt, duplicating skill metadata in the fixed prompt budget.

This patch removes the OpenClaw `## Skills` system-prompt section only when the Claude skills plugin was actually prepared for the run. Non-Claude CLI runs and Claude runs without a materialized plugin keep the prompt fallback unchanged.

## Real environment tested

Local source checkout on Linux, Node/pnpm workspace dependencies installed with `pnpm install --offline --ignore-scripts`.

Current head: `57cae48402`.

## Exact steps or command run after this patch

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/cli-runner/execute.ts src/agents/cli-runner/claude-skills-system-prompt.ts src/agents/cli-runner/claude-skills-system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsx -e "import { stripOpenClawSkillsPromptSection } from './src/agents/cli-runner/claude-skills-system-prompt.ts'; const prompt = ['A','','## Skills','<available_skills>weather</available_skills>','','## Memory','B'].join('\\n'); const out = stripOpenClawSkillsPromptSection(prompt); if (out.includes('available_skills') || out.includes('## Skills') || out !== 'A\\n\\n## Memory\\nB') throw new Error(out); console.log(out);"`

## Evidence after fix

- New helper coverage documents removing only the OpenClaw `## Skills` section while preserving the next section.
- The existing Claude skills plugin spawn test now builds a system prompt containing `<available_skills>`, asserts the spawned Claude command still has `--plugin-dir`, and asserts the written system-prompt file no longer contains `## Skills`, `<available_skills>`, or the skill description.
- The direct `tsx` check printed `A\n\n## Memory\nB`, proving the helper strips the skills block and preserves following sections.
- `git diff --check` completed with no output.
- `oxlint` completed with exit code 0 for all touched files.

## Observed result after fix

When the Claude skills plugin is available, Claude receives skills through its native plugin mechanism and the OpenClaw system prompt no longer duplicates the same skills catalog.

## What was not tested

I did not run a live Claude CLI token-count smoke locally. CI should run the normal targeted test suite for the touched CLI runner paths.
target.author: alix-007
